name: Dynamical supersymmetry breaking
summary: Supersymmetry is broken non-perturbatively by strong-gauge-dynamics effects in a hidden sector, generating the SUSY-breaking scale dynamically via dimensional transmutation rather than introducing it by hand. Naturally produces an exponentially small SUSY-breaking scale (Lambda_SUSY ~ M_Pl exp(-c/g^2)) without fine-tuning. Provides a UV-clean origin for the soft masses transmitted to the visible sector through gauge or gravity mediation.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides a natural origin for the SUSY-breaking scale at TeV - PeV via dimensional transmutation, completing the SUSY answer to the hierarchy problem. Builds on the SUSY framework; specific DSB sectors (3-2 model, ITIY, ISS) are the canonical examples.

new_scales: DSB confinement scale Lambda_DSB ~ TeV - 10^9 GeV
introduces.note: Hidden gauge group, matter content, and one mass scale. The exponential-suppression structure means this scale is naturally small without fine-tuning.

history: Witten (1981), 'Dynamical breaking of supersymmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 188, 513 — first DSB construction. Affleck, Dine, Seiberg (1985), 'Dynamical supersymmetry breaking in four dimensions and its phenomenological implications', Nucl. Phys. B 256, 557 — comprehensive program for DSB phenomenology. Intriligator-Seiberg-Shih (2006, arXiv:hep-th/0602239) revived DSB through metastable vacua. Foundational for all SUSY-breaking-mediation mechanisms.

references.citation: Witten, 'Dynamical breaking of supersymmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 188, 513 (1981)
Affleck, Dine, Seiberg, 'Dynamical supersymmetry breaking in four dimensions and its phenomenological implications', Nucl. Phys. B 256, 557 (1985)
